# Review: `deepmind/1133.lean` тАФ Erd┼Сs Problem 1133

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd┼Сs Problem 1133`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source: `[erdosproblems.com/1133](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1133)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (Erd┼Сs's result on polynomials bounded at sample points vs. bounded on [-1,1], and the stronger open conjecture).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1133 ... end Erdos1133`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1133`.
- [x] `open Polynomial Finset Set` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A тАФ no helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_1133` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 41]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) describing the problem statement in mathematical English.
- [ ] Docstring does not reference specific source papers (e.g., no `[Er...]` citation). This is a soft recommendation ("should ideally"), not a hard failure.

### `answer()` usage

The problem is stated as a direct assertion ("There exists ╬╡ > 0 such that ..."), which is **Case 3** тАФ no `answer()` wrapper needed.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly, with no `answer()` wrapper. Correct for Case 3.
- [x] All quantifiers (`тИА C`, `тИГ ╬╡`, `тИА n`, `тИА x`, `тИГ y`, `тИА P`, `тИГ t`) are in the theorem body, not as theorem parameters. Correct.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
- [x] Value is a valid category: `research open`.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 41`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed: `41`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (only one number, trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `41` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Approximations and expansions).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses multi-line `by sorry` format (`:= by` on last line of statement, `sorry` indented on next line). Acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (no definitions present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A тАФ no variants present. The file contains only the main theorem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `тИА`, `тИГ`, `тИИ`, `тДЭ`, `тДХ`, `тЙд`, `тЙе`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified in this review (requires `lake build`).

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)

The file is clean and fully conformant. The only soft note is that the theorem docstring does not cite a specific paper reference (e.g., an `[Er...]` tag), but the checklist marks this as "should ideally" rather than a hard requirement. All structural, naming, attribute, and style requirements are satisfied.

    11тЖТdistributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12тЖТWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13тЖТSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14тЖТlimitations under the License.
    15тЖТ-/
    16тЖТ
    17тЖТimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18тЖТ
    19тЖТ/-!
    20тЖТ# Erd┼Сs Problem 1133
    21тЖТ
    22тЖТ*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1133](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1133)
    23тЖТ
    24тЖТErd┼Сs proved that, for any $C > 0$, there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that if $n$ is sufficiently
    25тЖТlarge and $m = \lfloor(1+\varepsilon)n\rfloor$ then for any $x_1, \ldots, x_m \in [-1,1]$ there is
    26тЖТa polynomial $P$ of degree $n$ such that $|P(x_i)| \le 1$ for $1 \le i \le m$ and
    27тЖТ$\max_{x \in [-1,1]} |P(x)| > C$.
    28тЖТThe conjectured statement would also imply this, but Erd┼Сs said he could not even prove
    29тЖТit for $m = n$.
    30тЖТ-/
    31тЖТ
    32тЖТopen Polynomial Finset Set
    33тЖТ
    34тЖТnamespace Erdos1133
    35тЖТ
    36тЖТ/--
    37тЖТErd┼Сs Problem 1133:
    38тЖТLet $C > 0$. There exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that for sufficiently large $n$, for any
    39тЖТ$x_1, \ldots, x_n \in [-1,1]$ there exist $y_1, \ldots, y_n \in [-1,1]$ such that any polynomial
    40тЖТ$P$ of degree $< (1+\varepsilon)n$ that agrees with $y_i$ at $x_i$ for at least $(1-\varepsilon)n$
    41тЖТindices $i$ must satisfy $\max_{x \in [-1,1]} |P(x)| > C$.
    42тЖТ-/
    43тЖТ@[category research open, AMS 41]
    44тЖТtheorem erdos_1133 :
    45тЖТ    тИА C : тДЭ, C > 0 тЖТ
    46тЖТ    тИГ ╬╡ : тДЭ, ╬╡ > 0 тИз
    47тЖТ    тИГ N : тДХ, тИА n : тДХ, N тЙд n тЖТ
    48тЖТ    тИА x : Fin n тЖТ тДЭ, (тИА i, x i тИИ Icc (-1 : тДЭ) 1) тЖТ
    49тЖТ    тИГ y : Fin n тЖТ тДЭ, (тИА i, y i тИИ Icc (-1 : тДЭ) 1) тИз
    50тЖТ    тИА P : Polynomial тДЭ, (P.natDegree : тДЭ) < (1 + ╬╡) * n тЖТ
    51тЖТ    ((univ.filter (fun i : Fin n => P.eval (x i) = y i)).card : тДЭ) тЙе (1 - ╬╡) * n тЖТ
    52тЖТ    тИГ t тИИ Icc (-1 : тДЭ) 1, |P.eval t| > C := by
    53тЖТ  sorry
    54тЖТ
    55тЖТend Erdos1133
    56тЖТ

/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd┼Сs Problem 1133

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1133](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1133)

Erd┼Сs proved that, for any $C > 0$, there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that if $n$ is sufficiently
large and $m = \lfloor(1+\varepsilon)n\rfloor$ then for any $x_1, \ldots, x_m \in [-1,1]$ there is
a polynomial $P$ of degree $n$ such that $|P(x_i)| \le 1$ for $1 \le i \le m$ and
$\max_{x \in [-1,1]} |P(x)| > C$.
The conjectured statement would also imply this, but Erd┼Сs said he could not even prove
it for $m = n$.
-/

open Polynomial Finset Set

namespace Erdos1133

/--
Erd┼Сs Problem 1133:
Let $C > 0$. There exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that for sufficiently large $n$, for any
$x_1, \ldots, x_n \in [-1,1]$ there exist $y_1, \ldots, y_n \in [-1,1]$ such that any polynomial
$P$ of degree $< (1+\varepsilon)n$ that agrees with $y_i$ at $x_i$ for at least $(1-\varepsilon)n$
indices $i$ must satisfy $\max_{x \in [-1,1]} |P(x)| > C$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 41]
theorem erdos_1133 :
    тИА C : тДЭ, C > 0 тЖТ
    тИГ ╬╡ : тДЭ, ╬╡ > 0 тИз
    тИГ N : тДХ, тИА n : тДХ, N тЙд n тЖТ
    тИА x : Fin n тЖТ тДЭ, (тИА i, x i тИИ Icc (-1 : тДЭ) 1) тЖТ
    тИГ y : Fin n тЖТ тДЭ, (тИА i, y i тИИ Icc (-1 : тДЭ) 1) тИз
    тИА P : Polynomial тДЭ, (P.natDegree : тДЭ) < (1 + ╬╡) * n тЖТ
    ((univ.filter (fun i : Fin n => P.eval (x i) = y i)).card : тДЭ) тЙе (1 - ╬╡) * n тЖТ
    тИГ t тИИ Icc (-1 : тДЭ) 1, |P.eval t| > C := by
  sorry

end Erdos1133

content.text: The review summary says **PASS**. No changes needed.
